mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, directly increasing the present value of S&P 500 cash flows and generating positive forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or positive return frequency drops below 50% during rate cut periods.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.2 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions and weakening consumer spending capacity, triggering immediate risk-off positioning and negative S&P 500 returns within one week.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE increases by > 0.2 percentage points month-over-month
failure_condition: Forward 5-day returns remain positive in > 55% of triggered instances, or the signal loses statistical significance with p > 0.10 in out-of-sample backtests.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real personal consumption expenditures and housing starts indicates synchronized strength in consumer demand and residential investment, driving corporate revenue growth and producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to outperform a single-series baseline, with interaction term coefficient statistically indistinguishable from zero in quarterly return regressions.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production indices reflect expanding manufacturing capacity utilization and supply-side momentum, which translates into higher corporate profit margins and positive equity returns, but only during periods of stable inflation expectations.
failure_condition: Signal direction reverses or becomes statistically insignificant when CPIAUCSL month-over-month growth exceeds 3.0%, or 20-day forward returns underperform the market baseline by > 100 bps annually.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly real GDP growth surpassing 2.0% confirms robust aggregate demand and corporate earnings momentum, prompting institutional portfolio rebalancing into equities and generating positive S&P 500 returns within the following week.
failure_condition: Positive return frequency falls below 45% in triggered windows, or the signal exhibits negative autocorrelation indicating mean-reversion rather than momentum.

mechanism_narrative: Rising nonfarm payrolls combined with increasing federal funds rates indicate a tightening monetary response to labor market overheating, which compresses equity valuations and generates negative S&P 500 returns over the subsequent quarter.
failure_condition: The interaction effect fails to predict returns, with joint signal IC < 0.01 over rolling 3-year windows, or equity returns remain positive despite tightening conditions.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month increases in total nonfarm payrolls directly correlate with rising household income and consumer spending power, supporting corporate revenue growth and producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Rank IC remains negative or near zero across all rolling 2-year windows, or positive return frequency drops below 50% during payroll growth periods.

mechanism_narrative: A decline in industrial production alongside accelerating inflation signals stagflationary pressures that erode corporate profit margins and trigger rapid equity deleveraging, causing negative S&P 500 returns within one week.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL month-over-month change exceeds 0.4% while INDPRO declines
failure_condition: Forward 5-day returns are positive in > 60% of triggered instances, or the signal loses predictive power when volatility indices exceed 25.
